questions.question: What is the strongest conclusion from a passing test?
options: The code is correct in all environments
The checked claim was not falsified under tested assumptions
The feature is production-safe
All edge cases are covered
questions.explanation: A passing test only tells you no counterexample was found in the exercised space and assumptions. It is scoped evidence, not universal correctness.

questions.question: Why can a green CI still hide critical defects?
options: CI machines are slower than production
Green CI only means executed checks did not detect failures
CI tools ignore network-related bugs by design
Unit tests are always unreliable
questions.explanation: CI status reflects only what was checked. If claims, environments, or interleavings were not represented, serious defects can remain undetected.

questions.question: A team wants confidence against duplicate payment captures under retries. Which strategy is best aligned with this lesson?
options: Add one happy-path E2E test
Increase line coverage target from 80% to 95%
Define an idempotency invariant and combine property tests with concurrency integration tests
Rely on manual QA in staging
questions.explanation: The lesson emphasizes claim-first testing with complementary evidence. An explicit invariant plus multiple test modalities directly targets the failure mode.

questions.question: What is the primary value of recording testing assumptions?
options: To make docs longer
To justify skipped tests
To expose conditions required for claim validity and reduce hidden risk
To avoid writing integration tests
questions.explanation: Assumptions define the boundaries of your evidence. Making them explicit allows review, monitoring, and future hardening when conditions change.

questions.question: Which statement best distinguishes falsification from proof in routine software testing?
options: Falsification and proof are equivalent if coverage is high
Falsification finds concrete counterexamples, while passing tests usually do not prove universal correctness
Proof is what integration tests provide
Falsification only applies to security testing
questions.explanation: Most practical testing is falsification-oriented: failures are decisive, passes are provisional. Universal proof generally requires stronger formal methods and precise specifications.

_meta.title: Why We Test (and what tests don't prove)
